posts.title: Forced into a Care Home: This Could Have Been Me
posts.author: Sarah Rose
posts.excerpt: This post first appeared on the blog of the Pathfinders Neuromuscular Alliance — in it, Sarah Rose, their Chair of Trustees, writes about the importance of independent living and support from carers, and her fear that this could be taken away from her and others with complex disabilities:

posts.author: Garry Perry
posts.excerpt: (This is part of our Hearing Patient and Carer Voices series from Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust and Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust.) Garry Perry is a leading voice in patient experience and co-design across The Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust and Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust. Creator of the award-winning Little Voices programme, he champions approaches that put lived experience at the heart of improvement, shaping work…

posts.excerpt: (This is part of our Hearing Patient and Carer Voices series from Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust and Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust.) Andrew Rice is Head of Patient Voice across Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust and The Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust. His work focuses on ensuring that patients, families, and carers are not only heard, but actively shape how care is delivered and improved. He is a strong advocate for…

posts.title: Hearing Patient and Carer Voices
posts.author: Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust and Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust
posts.excerpt: The Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust and Walsall Healthcare NHS Trust are making great progress developing the Partners-in-Care ethos as well as refreshing their John's Campaign pledges (see video) . Here they present a three-part series of pieces giving 'what's, 'why's and 'how's about the changes they're making:

posts.title: “If You Can’t Help Your Own, Who Can You Help”
posts.author: Jessica Hayes
posts.excerpt: Jessica Hayes is a student occupational therapist at Teesside University. She discovered John's Campaign while supporting her beloved Nana in the last days of her life. Jessica recently became a John's Campaign ambassador. Here she tells us about her Nana, and why the campaign matters to her:
